item.title: Environmental and Lifestyle Factors in Inflammatory Bowel Diseases.
item.description: American journal of lifestyle medicine - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic, multifactorial disease resulting from complex interactions between the immune system, gut microbiota, genetic susceptibility, and environmental factor...

item.title: Gut microbial bile salt hydrolase as a metabolic gatekeeper in digestive homeostasis and disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - The gut microbiota exerts broad control over host physiology through tightly coordinated metabolic networks. Among these, bile salt hydrolase (BSH), a microbial enzyme, serves as a key mediator of ...

item.title: Altered Serum Concentrations of Fat-Soluble Vitamins in Dogs with Inflammatory Protein-Losing Enteropathy.
item.description: Animals : an open access journal from MDPI - Alterations in fat-soluble vitamins are well documented in humans with inflammatory bowel disease; however, data in dogs with chronic enteropathy and inflammatory protein-losing enteropathy (iPLE) ...
